packs.label: RepoPlanner .planning (reference tree)
packs.description: Checked-in .planning/*.xml + AGENTS.md from the RepoPlanner repository; bundled for the landing read-only cockpit and init download.

files.content:
[planning]
sprintSize = 5
currentProfile = "human"
conventionsPaths = ["AGENTS.md", "REQUIREMENTS.md", ".planning/AGENTS.md"]

[profiles.human]
description = "Default human view."

[profiles.agent]
description = "Agent perspective; defaultJson = true."
defaultJson = true

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<state>
  <agent-registry />
  <current-phase>07</current-phase>
  <current-plan>repo-planner-landing-docs</current-plan>
  <status>active</status>
  <next-action>Iterate on landing depth: optional cockpit panels for DECISIONS/REQUIREMENTS counts, more diagrams, and keeping this tree aligned with high-signal portfolio docs when they change.</next-action>
  <references>
    <reference>.planning/ROADMAP.xml</reference>
    <reference>.planning/TASK-REGISTRY.xml</reference>
    <reference>.planning/REQUIREMENTS.xml</reference>
    <reference>.planning/DECISIONS.xml</reference>
    <reference>.planning/AGENTS.md</reference>
    <reference>apps/landing (reference site)</reference>
  </references>
  <agent-id-policy>
    <format>agent-YYYYMMDD-xxxx</format>
    <rule>Optional: register a unique id in STATE.xml before claiming tasks if you use CLI task-update.</rule>
  </agent-id-policy>
</state>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<task-registry>
  <phase id="01">
    <task id="01-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Align monorepo scripts and docs with repo-planner CLI entrypoints.</goal>
      <keywords>cli,docs,integration</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run lint</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
    <task id="01-02" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Define embed boundaries: read-only by default, no casual server writes to git.</goal>
      <keywords>embed,security,policy</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run build</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="02">
    <task id="02-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Stabilize repo-planner/host exports for PlanningCockpitDashboard and workspace shell.</goal>
      <keywords>host,exports,react</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run lint</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="03">
    <task id="03-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Implement Pack vs Live mode split with explicit labels and separate data sources.</goal>
      <keywords>pack,live,cockpit</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run test</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="04">
    <task id="04-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Generate static builtin-packs.json from committed planning files for demo and docs.</goal>
      <keywords>builtin-pack,json,static</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>node apps/landing/scripts/build-builtin-packs.cjs</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="05">
    <task id="05-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Wire snapshot scoring and workflow cards to shared planning-workflow engine.</goal>
      <keywords>snapshot,workflow,sprint</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run lint</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="06">
    <task id="06-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Structured inspector views for task-registry, roadmap rows, and decisions with host policy badges.</goal>
      <keywords>forms,inspector,xml</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run build</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
  <phase id="07">
    <task id="07-01" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Maintain .planning/*.xml at RepoPlanner repo root as canonical source for landing builtin pack.</goal>
      <keywords>planning-xml,landing,builtin</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>node apps/landing/scripts/build-builtin-packs.cjs</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
    <task id="07-02" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>CockpitFromPack: parse ROADMAP, STATE, TASK-REGISTRY with fast-xml-parser; read-only list UI matching mock chrome.</goal>
      <keywords>fast-xml-parser,cockpit,client</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>npm run build</command>
      </commands>
      <depends>07-01</depends>
    </task>
    <task id="07-03" agent-id="" status="done">
      <goal>Document on landing: planning-pack JSON shape, parsing pipeline, and difference from semantic embeddings.</goal>
      <keywords>docs,parsing,pack-json</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>npm run build</command>
      </commands>
      <depends>07-02</depends>
    </task>
    <task id="07-04" agent-id="" status="planned">
      <goal>Migrate rp-* skill stubs to gad:* in get-anything-done; keep RepoPlanner as format reference.</goal>
      <keywords>gad,skills,migration</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run lint</command>
      </commands>
      <depends></depends>
    </task>
    <task id="07-05" agent-id="" status="planned">
      <goal>Port host-heavy cockpit features to GAD-owned package for consumers that standardize on gad CLI.</goal>
      <keywords>gad-ui,embed,future</keywords>
      <commands>
        <command>pnpm run build</command>
      </commands>
      <depends>07-04</depends>
    </task>
  </phase>
</task-registry>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<roadmap>
  <phase id="01">
    <title>Portfolio integration baseline</title>
    <goal>Monorepo alignment, CLI docs, embed boundaries, preview uploads, route-installer cleanup for a host app that embeds RepoPlanner.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends></depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="02">
    <title>Package host contract</title>
    <goal>Cleaner package-level host contract so other Next.js or Vite apps can import repo-planner/host with stable props.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends>01</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="03">
    <title>Live cockpit</title>
    <goal>Real cockpit body, Pack vs Live modes, shared bundle plumbing, dashboard chrome — one data contract for file tree and inspector.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends>02</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="04">
    <title>Built-in planning-pack generation</title>
    <goal>Ship builtin-packs.json (or equivalent) from repo-owned planning files so static sites can demo without a backend.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends>03</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="05">
    <title>Workflow loop</title>
    <goal>Sprint-aware snapshot scoring, kickoff scaffolding, done gates, ownership guidance — same engine for CLI and UI.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends>04</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="06">
    <title>Structured cockpit views</title>
    <goal>Form-based editing where schema is known, badges, autosave in pack mode, immutable ids, host policy flags.</goal>
    <status>done</status>
    <depends>05</depends>
  </phase>
  <phase id="07">
    <title>GAD migration and reference site</title>
    <goal>Skills and CLI narrative move toward Get Anything Done; this repository stays a file-format and UI reference; landing site documents parsing, packs, and read-only cockpit behavior.</goal>
    <status>active</status>
    <depends>06</depends>
  </phase>
</roadmap>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<decisions>
  <decision id="RP-LANDING-01">
    <title>Reference site cockpit is read-only parsed XML</title>
    <summary>The RepoPlanner apps/landing /cockpit route loads builtin-packs.json, then parses ROADMAP.xml, STATE.xml, and TASK-REGISTRY.xml with fast-xml-parser. List rows reflect committed task status attributes — not hand-written mock rows.</summary>
    <impact>Operators see how a host app can treat planning files as data without a database.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="RP-LANDING-02">
    <title>Canonical demo planning tree lives in repository .planning/</title>
    <summary>The same XML under RepoPlanner/.planning is bundled into public/planning-embed/builtin-packs.json at build time and mirrored into the downloadable init bundle under public/init/planning for parity.</summary>
    <impact>One source of truth for docs, demo, and planning init samples.</impact>
  </decision>
  <decision id="RP-PARSE-01">
    <title>Client parsing uses fast-xml-parser, not embeddings</title>
    <summary>“Fast” in this stack refers to fast-xml-parser (attribute-aware XML to JS objects). Semantic search or vector embeddings are a separate concern and are not required for the cockpit list views.</summary>
    <impact>Avoid confusing XML parsing with embedding pipelines when reading architecture notes.</impact>
  </decision>
</decisions>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<errors-and-attempts>
  <!-- Reserved for failed approaches while evolving landing + pack pipeline. -->
</errors-and-attempts>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<requirements>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-01" status="active">
    <statement>Roadmap-first loop: ROADMAP.xml phases, STATE.xml next-action, TASK-REGISTRY.xml tasks stay the machine source of truth.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-02" status="active">
    <statement>Host embeds default read-only; server writes to git require explicit operator policy and env flags.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-03" status="active">
    <statement>Pack mode and Live mode must never be confused — labels, data source, and export paths are explicit.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-04" status="active">
    <statement>Parsing for XML planning files is shared between CLI (planning-parse-core) and hosts so UI and snapshot agree on structure.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-05" status="active">
    <statement>Snapshot and cockpit prioritize structured steering (phases, tasks, risks) over raw document dumps.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-06" status="active">
    <statement>AGENTS.md remains the human playbook; the cockpit shows a compact reminder and deep link, not a full inline paste.</statement>
  </requirement>
  <requirement id="REPOPLAN-REQ-07" status="active">
    <statement>Static demos may ship a JSON pack (virtual paths + file contents) parsed in-browser for roadmap/state/tasks — no fake rows that are not in XML.</statement>
  </requirement>
</requirements>

# Agent loop — skillless Ralph

This is a **skillless** take on the **Ralph Wiggum loop**: the agent reads durable state from disk, does **one** concrete task, updates the XML/Markdown record, and commits. There is **no** skills framework here — no trigger files, no skill packs — only **files + optional CLI + optional cockpit** in a host app.

## This repository’s `.planning/`

The XML in **`RepoPlanner/.planning/`** is **real planning data**, not a toy stub. It describes the product phases (portfolio-style integration history through **07 · GAD migration and reference site**), current **STATE.xml** next-action, and **TASK-REGISTRY.xml** rows. The **RepoPlanner reference site** (`apps/landing`) bundles these files into **`public/planning-embed/builtin-packs.json`** at build time. The **`/cockpit`** page fetches that JSON and parses **`ROADMAP.xml`**, **`STATE.xml`**, and **`TASK-REGISTRY.xml`** in the browser with **fast-xml-parser** — read-only list views, same chrome as the static mock.

For **Get Anything Done** as the active CLI and skills stack, treat this tree as **format + UX reference**; migrate execution to `gad` in your host repo per your own `.planning/` and `gad-config.toml`.

## Where it fits

RepoPlanner is **especially comfortable on existing repos**: real directories, tests, CI, and git history give tasks meaningful verification commands. You can use the same loop on a young repo too — the point is to keep **one** `.planning/` home for roadmap, state, and tasks instead of scattering notes across issues and ad hoc markdown.

## Loop (every iteration)

1. **Snapshot** — run your host’s planning CLI (`snapshot` / `checklist`) or open the cockpit so the current phase and next action are obvious.
2. **Pick** — choose exactly one `planned` task in `TASK-REGISTRY.xml` (or split work until you can).
3. **Implement** — touch the smallest surface that completes the task; run the listed verification commands.
4. **Record** — update `STATE.xml`, task status, `DECISIONS.xml` if you changed approach, `ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml` if something failed.
5. **Commit** — one commit per task when possible (discipline for per-task traceability).

## Where narrative lives

- **REQUIREMENTS.md** (repo root) — long-form scope when you use the classic layout.
- **REQUIREMENTS.xml** (optional, under `.planning/`) — machine-oriented requirement ids for tools that read XML only.
- **.planning/*.xml** — machine-friendly timeline, tasks, state — the cockpit and CLI summarize these.

## Optional CLI

Add a `package.json` script pointing at `repo-planner` / `loop-cli.mjs` if you want agents to run `snapshot` without opening the UI.
